title: Integrated Population Viability Analysis (PVA): conservation portfolio evaluation
text:
Anna Ortega
Cooperative Institute for Marine & Atmospheric Research, University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa, Honolulu, Hawaiʻi

Zachary Siders
Fisheries and Aquatic Sciences Program, University of Florida, Gainesville, Florida

Summer Martin
Protected Species Division, Pacific Islands Fisheries Science Center, National Marine Fisheries Service, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, Honolulu, Hawaiʻi

title: Integrated Population Viability Analysis (PVA): conservation portfolio evaluation
text:
Overview
This vignette documents an integrated Population Viability Analysis (PVA) pipeline. The goal of this tool is to assess both the impact of human-wildlife interactions (“takes”) and the efficacy of proactive conservation strategies on the long-term survival of a species. It connects three distinct modeling phases:

Data Imputation: Filling gaps in historical nesting beach surveys using Bayesian state-space models via JAGS.
Demographic Translation: Implementing symmetric lifecycle algebra to convert “Takes” into “Adult Nester Equivalents” (ANE) to map retrospective demographic conversions.
Projection: Simulating future population trends over a 100-year evaluation horizon across 8 unique management tracks:

Track 1: Pristine baseline (Unimpacted counterfactual with no historical or future fishery take).
Track 2: Status Quo (Realized threats under current baseline fishery interaction levels).
Track 3: Option A (Nest protection / caging out predators).
Track 4: Option B (Juvenile headstarting to yearling releases).
Track 5: Option C (Cessation of beach poaching of mature nesting adult females).
Track 6: Option D (Gear avoidance/closures to mitigate interaction rates).
Track 7: Option E (Safe handling and circle hooks to reduce post-release mortality).
Track 8: Combined portfolio (Concurrent implementation of Options A–E).


The primary goals of this project are to (1) expand traditional population assessments to capture the positive impact of diverse conservation actions, and (2) provide an open-access, fully automated workflow for this population assessment method to be easily reproduced and adapted for other species or regions.

R code
R script files to execute the population assessments described here can be found in the GitHub repository for this webpage.


License
As a work by US federal employees as part of their official duties, this project is in the public domain within the United States of America. Additionally, we waive copyright and related rights in the work worldwide through the CC0 1.0 Universal public domain dedication.


NOAA Disclaimer
This repository is a scientific product and is not official communication of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, or the United States Department of Commerce. All NOAA GitHub project code is provided on an ‘as is’ basis and the user assumes responsibility for its use. Any claims against the Department of Commerce or Department of Commerce bureaus stemming from the use of this GitHub project will be governed by all applicable Federal law. Any reference to specific commercial products, processes, or services by service mark, trademark, manufacturer, or otherwise, does not constitute or imply their endorsement, recommendation or favoring by the Department of Commerce. The Department of Commerce seal and logo, or the seal and logo of a DOC bureau, shall not be used in any manner to imply endorsement of any commercial product or activity by DOC or the United States Government.

title: Phase IV: The 8-Scenario Simulator
text:
#| label: load-state
#| echo: false
load("pva_state.RData")
We simulate forward for r horizon years across 8 independent arrays, isolating specific intervention impacts alongside a combined portfolio strategy.
#| label: simulator-loop
#| code-summary: "100-Year Forward Simulation Loop (8 Tracks)"
#| cache: true

# ==============================================================================
# 5. 8-Scenario Simulator
# ==============================================================================
message(">>> Running 100-year forward portfolio engine over 8 independent strategy arrays...")

# --- ALLOCATE EXPANDED 8-Scenario ARRAY SETS ---
sim_notake   <- matrix(NA, n_sims, horizon) # Scenario 1: Unimpacted pristine baseline
sim_take     <- matrix(NA, n_sims, horizon) # Scenario 2: Status Quo (Current threat, fishery take)
sim_optA     <- matrix(NA, n_sims, horizon) # Scenario 3: Nest protections
sim_optB     <- matrix(NA, n_sims, horizon) # Scenario 4: Juvenile headstarting
sim_optC     <- matrix(NA, n_sims, horizon) # Scenario 5: Adult poaching ban
sim_optD     <- matrix(NA, n_sims, horizon) # Scenario 6: Gear avoidance mitigation
sim_optE     <- matrix(NA, n_sims, horizon) # Scenario 7: Safe handling
sim_combined <- matrix(NA, n_sims, horizon) # Scenario 8: Combined management portfolio

# kickoff of the loop with baseline starting population, all use real-world baseline except Scenario 1: pristine (no fishery)
for(i in 1:n_sims) {
  # Assign track initial steps
  curr_nt <- start_pop_draws_adj[i]
  curr_t  <- curr_A <- curr_B <- curr_C <- curr_D <- curr_E <- curr_comb <- start_pop_draws[i]

  # Create empty deferred vectors for tracking dynamic timeline modifications
  vec_loss_sq        <- rep(0, horizon) # Status Quo loss stream
  vec_loss_optE      <- rep(0, horizon) # Reduced mortality (safe handling) loss stream
  vec_gain_optA      <- rep(0, horizon) # Nest caging returns
  vec_gain_optB      <- rep(0, horizon) # Headstart yearling returns
  vec_gain_optC      <- rep(0, horizon) # Adult protection returns

  # Pre-calculate yearling returns for Option A (Caging)
  yearlings_produced_optA <- optA_nests_saved * sp_clutch_size * sp_hatch_success * sp_hatch_to_yearling

  for(y in 1:horizon) {

    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Scenario 1: Demography Track (No-Take Baseline)
    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
    curr_nt <- rnorm(1, curr_nt * exp(mock_posteriors_adj$U[i]), sqrt(mock_posteriors_adj$Q[i]))
    sim_notake[i,y] <- curr_nt

    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Calculate fishery losses
    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Status Quo Take Engine (Tracks 2, 3, 4, 5)
    takes_sq <- rCMP(1, mu = cmp_mu, nu = cmp_nu)
    if (takes_sq > 0) {
      for(t in 1:takes_sq) {
        future_len <- rnorm(1, mean = 75, sd = 8) # calculates size
        age_t <- sp_tknot - (1 / sp_k) * log(1 - (pmin(future_len, sp_linf - 0.1) / sp_linf)) # calculates age
        if(is.nan(age_t) || is.na(age_t)) age_t <- sp_max_age - 2

        rem_yrs   <- y:horizon; l_y <- length(rem_yrs)
        ages_traj <- seq(age_t, length.out = l_y, by = 1)
        lens_traj <- ifelse(ages_traj >= sp_max_age, rep(sp_linf * 0.99, l_y), sp_linf * (1 - exp(-sp_k * (ages_traj - sp_tknot))))
        p_mat     <- 1.0 / (1.0 + exp(-(lens_traj - sp_lmat) / sp_sig_mat)) # when it would have matured
        p_binom   <- rbinom(l_y, 1, p_mat)
        if(any(p_binom == 1)) p_binom[min(which(p_binom == 1)):l_y] <- 1

        surv_vector <- cumprod((1 - p_binom) * sp_ane_pj + p_binom * sp_ane_pa) # likelihood of surviving natural threats
        future_mort <- runif(1, 0.3, 0.7)
        sr          <- surv_vector * p_binom * sp_pf * future_mort * (1 / sp_remig_int)
        vec_loss_sq[y:horizon] <- vec_loss_sq[y:horizon] + sr # writes the lost reproductive value into the calendar
      }
    }

    # Gear avoidance mitigation (Scenario 6 - Option D)
    # catch rate dropped from 15 to 4.5
    takes_optD <- rCMP(1, mu = optD_cmp_mu_mitigated, nu = cmp_nu)
    vec_loss_optD_annual <- 0
    if (takes_optD > 0) {
      vec_loss_optD_temp <- rep(0, horizon)
      for(t in 1:takes_optD) {
        future_len <- rnorm(1, mean = 75, sd = 8)
        age_t <- sp_tknot - (1 / sp_k) * log(1 - (pmin(future_len, sp_linf - 0.1) / sp_linf))
        if(is.nan(age_t) || is.na(age_t)) age_t <- sp_max_age - 2

        rem_yrs   <- y:horizon; l_y <- length(rem_yrs)
        ages_traj <- seq(age_t, length.out = l_y, by = 1)
        lens_traj <- ifelse(ages_traj >= sp_max_age, rep(sp_linf * 0.99, l_y), sp_linf * (1 - exp(-sp_k * (ages_traj - sp_tknot))))
        p_mat     <- 1.0 / (1.0 + exp(-(lens_traj - sp_lmat) / sp_sig_mat))
        p_binom   <- rbinom(l_y, 1, p_mat)
        if(any(p_binom == 1)) p_binom[min(which(p_binom == 1)):l_y] <- 1

        surv_vector <- cumprod((1 - p_binom) * sp_ane_pj + p_binom * sp_ane_pa)
        future_mort <- runif(1, 0.3, 0.7)
        sr          <- surv_vector * p_binom * sp_pf * future_mort * (1 / sp_remig_int)
        vec_loss_optD_temp[y:horizon] <- vec_loss_optD_temp[y:horizon] + sr
      }
      vec_loss_optD_annual <- vec_loss_optD_temp[y]
    }

    # Safe handling (Scenario 7 - Option E)
    # Hooking mortality rate changes from 30-70% to 5-18%
    if (takes_sq > 0) {
      for(t in 1:takes_sq) {
        future_len <- rnorm(1, mean = 75, sd = 8)
        age_t <- sp_tknot - (1 / sp_k) * log(1 - (pmin(future_len, sp_linf - 0.1) / sp_linf))
        if(is.nan(age_t) || is.na(age_t)) age_t <- sp_max_age - 2

        rem_yrs   <- y:horizon; l_y <- length(rem_yrs)
        ages_traj <- seq(age_t, length.out = l_y, by = 1)
        lens_traj <- ifelse(ages_traj >= sp_max_age, rep(sp_linf * 0.99, l_y), sp_linf * (1 - exp(-sp_k * (ages_traj - sp_tknot))))
        p_mat     <- 1.0 / (1.0 + exp(-(lens_traj - sp_lmat) / sp_sig_mat))
        p_binom   <- rbinom(l_y, 1, p_mat)
        if(any(p_binom == 1)) p_binom[min(which(p_binom == 1)):l_y] <- 1

        surv_vector    <- cumprod((1 - p_binom) * sp_ane_pj + p_binom * sp_ane_pa)
        future_mort_me <- runif(1, optE_mort_min, optE_mort_max) # Mitigated post-release mortality rate
        sr             <- surv_vector * p_binom * sp_pf * future_mort_me * (1 / sp_remig_int)
        vec_loss_optE[y:horizon] <- vec_loss_optE[y:horizon] + sr
      }
    }

    # -----------------------------
    # Calculate conservation gains
    # -----------------------------
    # Option A  (Nest Protection)
    if (yearlings_produced_optA > 0) { # earlier calculation to get from eggs to yearlings
      rem_yrs_A <- y:horizon; l_y_A <- length(rem_yrs_A)
      ages_A    <- seq(1.0, length.out = l_y_A, by = 1)
      lens_A    <- ifelse(ages_A >= sp_max_age, rep(sp_linf * 0.99, l_y_A), sp_linf * (1 - exp(-sp_k * (ages_A - sp_tknot))))
      p_mat_A   <- 1.0 / (1.0 + exp(-(lens_A - sp_lmat) / sp_sig_mat))
      p_binom_A <- rbinom(l_y_A, 1, p_mat_A)
      if(any(p_binom_A == 1)) p_binom_A[min(which(p_binom_A == 1)):l_y_A] <- 1
      surv_vector_A <- cumprod((1 - p_binom_A) * sp_ane_pj + p_binom_A * sp_ane_pa)
      vec_gain_optA[y:horizon] <- vec_gain_optA[y:horizon] + (surv_vector_A * p_binom_A * sp_pf * (1 / sp_remig_int) * yearlings_produced_optA)
    }

    # Option B (Headstarting) # earlier calculation to get from eggs to headstarted yearlings
    if (optB_headstarts_released > 0) {
      rem_yrs_B <- y:horizon; l_y_B <- length(rem_yrs_B)
      # Accelerate entry timeline: captive individuals enter directly at the release age
      ages_B    <- seq(optB_release_age, length.out = l_y_B, by = 1)
      lens_B    <- ifelse(ages_B >= sp_max_age, rep(sp_linf * 0.99, l_y_B), sp_linf * (1 - exp(-sp_k * (ages_B - sp_tknot))))
      p_mat_B   <- 1.0 / (1.0 + exp(-(lens_B - sp_lmat) / sp_sig_mat))
      p_binom_B <- rbinom(l_y_B, 1, p_mat_B)
      if(any(p_binom_B == 1)) p_binom_B[min(which(p_binom_B == 1)):l_y_B] <- 1
      surv_vector_B <- cumprod((1 - p_binom_B) * sp_ane_pj + p_binom_B * sp_ane_pa)
      vec_gain_optB[y:horizon] <- vec_gain_optB[y:horizon] + (surv_vector_B * p_binom_B * sp_pf * (1 / sp_remig_int) * optB_headstarts_released)
    }

    # Option C (Adult nester protection)
    if (optC_adults_saved > 0) {
      rem_yrs_C <- y:horizon; l_y_C <- length(rem_yrs_C)
      # Zero development time-lag: Saved beach adults return immediate reproductive dividends
      ages_C    <- seq(35.0, length.out = l_y_C, by = 1)
      lens_C    <- rep(sp_linf * 0.99, l_y_C)
      p_binom_C <- rep(1, l_y_C) # Adults are 100% mature at start
      surv_vector_C <- cumprod(p_binom_C * sp_ane_pa)
      vec_gain_optC[y:horizon] <- vec_gain_optC[y:horizon] + (surv_vector_C * p_binom_C * (1 / sp_remig_int) * optC_adults_saved)
    }

    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Compute all the tracks for all scenarios
    # --------------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Scenario 2: Status Quo (SQ) - isolated threat only, here, fisheries mortality
    curr_t <- rnorm(1, (curr_t - vec_loss_sq[y]) * exp(mock_posteriors$U[i]), sqrt(mock_posteriors$Q[i]))
    sim_take[i,y] <- curr_t

    # Scenario 3: Option A (Nest protection vs SQ Threats)
    curr_A <- rnorm(1, (curr_A - vec_loss_sq[y] + vec_gain_optA[y]) * exp(mock_posteriors$U[i]), sqrt(mock_posteriors$Q[i]))
    sim_optA[i,y] <- curr_A

    # Scenario 4: Option B (Headstart vs SQ Threats)
    curr_B <- rnorm(1, (curr_B - vec_loss_sq[y] + vec_gain_optB[y]) * exp(mock_posteriors$U[i]), sqrt(mock_posteriors$Q[i]))
    sim_optB[i,y] <- curr_B

    # Scenario 5: Option C (Adult protection vs SQ Threats)
    curr_C <- rnorm(1, (curr_C - vec_loss_sq[y] + vec_gain_optC[y]) * exp(mock_posteriors$U[i]), sqrt(mock_posteriors$Q[i]))
    sim_optC[i,y] <- curr_C

    # Scenario 6: Option D (Gear avoidance mitigation)
    curr_D <- rnorm(1, (curr_D - vec_loss_optD_annual) * exp(mock_posteriors$U[i]), sqrt(mock_posteriors$Q[i]))
    sim_optD[i,y] <- curr_D

    # Scenario 7: Option E (Safe handling mitigation)
    curr_E <- rnorm(1, (curr_E - vec_loss_optE[y]) * exp(mock_posteriors$U[i]), sqrt(mock_posteriors$Q[i]))
    sim_optE[i,y] <- curr_E

    # Scenario 8: Combined Portfolio (All mitigation options together (nest protection, headstarting, adult protection, gear avoidance, and safe handling))
    curr_comb <- rnorm(1, (curr_comb - vec_loss_optE[y] + vec_gain_optA[y] + vec_gain_optB[y] + vec_gain_optC[y]) * exp(mock_posteriors$U[i]), sqrt(mock_posteriors$Q[i]))
    sim_combined[i,y] <- curr_comb
  }

  # Structural Absorbing Barriers (Locking crashed replicates cleanly to 0); you can't return from extirpation
  if(any(sim_notake[i,] < 0))   { sim_notake[i, min(which(sim_notake[i,] < 0)):horizon]   <- 0 }
  if(any(sim_take[i,] < 0))     { sim_take[i, min(which(sim_take[i,] < 0)):horizon]     <- 0 }
  if(any(sim_optA[i,] < 0))     { sim_optA[i, min(which(sim_optA[i,] < 0)):horizon]     <- 0 }
  if(any(sim_optB[i,] < 0))     { sim_optB[i, min(which(sim_optB[i,] < 0)):horizon]     <- 0 }
  if(any(sim_optC[i,] < 0))     { sim_optC[i, min(which(sim_optC[i,] < 0)):horizon]     <- 0 }
  if(any(sim_optD[i,] < 0))     { sim_optD[i, min(which(sim_optD[i,] < 0)):horizon]     <- 0 }
  if(any(sim_optE[i,] < 0))     { sim_optE[i, min(which(sim_optE[i,] < 0)):horizon]     <- 0 }
  if(any(sim_combined[i,] < 0)) { sim_combined[i, min(which(sim_combined[i,] < 0)):horizon] <- 0 }
}

save.image("pva_state.RData")

title: Phase II: Virtual Reality & Imputation
text:
#| label: load-state
#| echo: false
# Load variables from previous phase
load("pva_state.RData")
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(jagsUI)
Real-world data is messy. Here we generate synthetic data across three monitoring beaches and purposefully inject 30% missing data, which we will impute via a Bayesian state-space model.
#| label: mock-data-and-jags
#| code-summary: "Generate Synthetic Data & Run JAGS Imputation"
#| results: hide

# ====================================================================
# 2. Synthetic historical data generation matrix (making fake data)
# ====================================================================
message(">>> Constructing data matrices for synthetic portfolio evaluation...")

# Simulate tracking data lines across three sample monitoring nesting beaches, Alpha, Beta, Gamma
mock_abundance <- expand.grid(Year = hist_years, Site = c("Alpha_Beach", "Beta_Beach", "Gamma_Beach")) %>%
  mutate(
    True_Females = ifelse(Site == "Alpha_Beach", 450, ifelse(Site == "Beta_Beach", 280, 180)) * exp(-0.025 * (Year - 2000)) * runif(n(), 0.75, 1.25),
    Annual_Nests = round(True_Females * sp_clutch_freq)
  )

# Simulate fishery observer length and mortality measurements
mock_observer <- data.frame(
  Year = sort(sample(hist_years[hist_years >= 2005], 50, replace = TRUE)),
  Species = "Mock_Sea_Turtle",
  SCL_raw = round(rnorm(50, mean = 76.5, sd = 9.8), 1),
  M_mu = round(runif(50, min = 0.25, max = 0.75), 3)
)

# Simulate total fleet-wide interaction estimates (SAFE compliance accounts)
mock_safe <- data.frame(Year = hist_years) %>%
  mutate(Total_Est = ifelse(Year < 2005, 0, round(rnorm(n(), mean = 22, sd = 3))))

# ==============================================================================
# 2.5 Bayesian Seasonal Imputation of Missing Monthly Data
# ==============================================================================
message(">>> Generating monthly seasonal data, injecting gaps, and running JAGS imputation...")

# 1. Create a monthly dataset with a simulated seasonal wave (peak in summer)
months_total <- n_hist_yrs * 12
mock_monthly <- expand.grid(
  Month_Idx = 1:months_total,
  Site = c("Alpha_Beach", "Beta_Beach", "Gamma_Beach")
) %>%
  mutate(
    # Derive Year and Month mathematically so it maps perfectly to the 756 rows
    Year = min(hist_years) + floor((Month_Idx - 1) / 12),
    Month = (Month_Idx - 1) %% 12 + 1,

    # Base annual trend
    True_Base = ifelse(Site == "Alpha_Beach", 450,
                       ifelse(Site == "Beta_Beach", 280, 180)) * exp(-0.025 * (Year - min(hist_years))),

    # Add trigonometric seasonality: strong peak around month 6/7
    Seasonal_Multiplier = pmax(0.1, 1 + 0.8 * cos(2 * pi * (Month - 6.5) / 12)),
    True_Nests = (True_Base / 12) * Seasonal_Multiplier * runif(n(), 0.8, 1.2),

    # Poke holes to create missing data (30% missing)
    Observed_Nests = ifelse(runif(n()) < 0.30, NA, round(True_Nests))
  )

# Reshape for JAGS (Sites as rows, Months as columns)
obs_matrix <- mock_monthly %>%
  select(Month_Idx, Site, Observed_Nests) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Month_Idx, values_from = Observed_Nests) %>%
  select(-Site) %>%
  as.matrix()

# 2. Define the JAGS state-space model with seasonal harmonics
jags_seasonal_model <- "
model {
  for (i in 1:n_sites) {
    # Initial state
    X[i, 1] ~ dnorm(mu0, tau0)

    # Site-specific seasonal parameters
    beta_cos[i] ~ dnorm(0, 0.01)
    beta_sin[i] ~ dnorm(0, 0.01)

    for (t in 1:n_months) {
      # Trigonometric seasonal component
      seasonality[i, t] <- beta_cos[i] * cos(2 * pi * month_seq[t] / period) +
                           beta_sin[i] * sin(2 * pi * month_seq[t] / period)

      # Combined expected mean (underlying state + seasonality)
      mu_obs[i, t] <- X[i, t] + seasonality[i, t]
      Y[i, t] ~ dnorm(mu_obs[i, t], tau_obs)
    }

    # Random walk for the underlying annual trend
    for (t in 2:n_months) {
      X[i, t] ~ dnorm(X[i, t-1] + u, tau_proc)
    }
  }

  # Global Priors
  mu0 ~ dnorm(0, 0.001)
  tau0 <- pow(sigma0, -2)
  sigma0 ~ dunif(0, 100)

  u ~ dnorm(0, 0.001)
  tau_proc <- pow(sigma_proc, -2)
  sigma_proc ~ dunif(0, 100)

  tau_obs <- pow(sigma_obs, -2)
  sigma_obs ~ dunif(0, 100)
}
"

# 3. Bundle the monthly data
jags_data <- list(
  Y = log(obs_matrix + 1), # Log-transform to stabilize variance
  n_sites = nrow(obs_matrix),
  n_months = ncol(obs_matrix),
  month_seq = rep(1:12, times = n_hist_yrs),
  period = 12, # 12 months in a standard annual nesting cycle
  pi = pi
)

# Monitor the combined mean (mu_obs) to extract our imputed values
jags_params <- c("mu_obs", "beta_cos", "beta_sin", "u")

# 4. Run JAGS

# Write the model string to a temporary file so parallel workers can read it
model_file_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".txt")
writeLines(jags_seasonal_model, con = model_file_path)

out_jags <- jags(
  data = jags_data,
  parameters.to.save = jags_params,
  model.file = model_file_path, # Pass the file path instead of textConnection
  n.chains = 3,
  n.iter = 5000,
  n.burnin = 1000,
  n.thin = 2,
  parallel = TRUE,
  verbose = FALSE
)

# Clean up the temporary file afterward
unlink(model_file_path)

# 5. Extract the median imputed values
imputed_log_matrix <- out_jags$q50$mu_obs
imputed_nests <- exp(imputed_log_matrix) - 1

# 6. Merge imputed values and aggregate back into annual totals for the PVA
mock_abundance <- mock_monthly %>%
  arrange(Site, Month_Idx) %>%
  mutate(
    Imputed_Nests = pmax(0, as.vector(t(imputed_nests))), # Ensure no negative nests
    Final_Monthly_Nests = ifelse(is.na(Observed_Nests), Imputed_Nests, Observed_Nests)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Year, Site) %>%
  summarise(
    Annual_Nests = sum(Final_Monthly_Nests),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

message(">>> Seasonal imputation complete. Annual totals reconstructed for PVA.")
#| label: trend-plot
#| code-fold: false
#| fig-cap: "Manuscript Fig 1: Baseline Trend Fit across simulated nesting beaches."

# ===============================================
# 3. Baseline trend extraction from nesting data
# ===============================================
message(">>> Simulating state-space posterior variance structures...")

# extracts the background population growth rate (U) and process variance (Q)
mock_posteriors <- list(
  U = rnorm(n_sims, mean = -0.028, sd = 0.004), # Regional trend posterior distribution
  Q = rgamma(n_sims, shape = 2, rate = 45)      # Process noise variance distribution
)
start_pop_draws <- rnorm(n_sims, mean = 720, sd = 45) * sp_remig_int

# --- Manuscript figure, Trend Fit with three nesting beaches ---
p_fit <- ggplot(mock_abundance, aes(x = Year, y = log(Annual_Nests/sp_clutch_freq), color = Site)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) + geom_line(alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() + labs(y = "log(Annual Nesting Cohort)")

print(p_fit)
ggsave(filename = paste0(out_dir, "/Manuscript_Fig1_Trend_Fit.png"), plot = p_fit, width = 7, height = 4.5)

save.image("pva_state.RData")

title: Phase I: Initialization and Biological Constraints
text:
First, we load our core packages and establish the underlying Conway-Maxwell-Poisson (CMP) mathematical utilities that keep our equations stable.
#| label: setup-and-math
#| code-summary: "Load Packages & Define CMP Math Utilities"

# --- Load core packages ---
library(dplyr) # data reshaping
library(tidyr) # data reshaping
library(ggplot2) # plotting
library(mvtnorm) # statistical probability distributions
library(truncnorm) # statistical probability distributions
library(jagsUI) # Bayesian modeling

# --- Establish directory routing  ---
out_dir <- paste0(getwd(), "/EcolApps_Output")
if(!dir.exists(out_dir)) dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE)
message("Initialization successful. Directing output to: ", out_dir)


# ==============================================================================
# 0. Fixed CMP (Conway-Maxwell-Poisson) mathematical utilities
# ==============================================================================
# handles complex underlying calculus to keep equations stable
compute_CMP_constant <- function(Lambda, Nu, Mu, Tol, Max, Log=TRUE, Type="Z"){
  if( (!is.na(Lambda) & Lambda > 10^Nu) | (!is.na(Mu) & Mu^Nu > 10^Nu) ){
    if(Type=="Z"){ ln_Const = Nu*Lambda^(1/Nu) - ((Nu-1)/(2*Nu))*log(Lambda) - ((Nu-1)/2)*log(2*pi) - (1/2)*log(Nu) }
    if(Type=="S"){ ln_Const = Nu*Mu - ((Nu-1)/(2))*log(Mu) - ((Nu-1)/2)*log(2*pi) - (1/2)*log(Nu) }
  }else{
    Const = rep(0,Max+1); Index = 1; Const[Index] = 1
    while( Const[Index]/Const[1] > Tol ){
      if(Type=="Z") Const[Index+1] = Const[Index] * ( Lambda / Index^Nu )
      if(Type=="S") Const[Index+1] = Const[Index] * ( Mu / Index )^Nu; Index = Index + 1
    }
    ln_Const = log(sum(Const))
  }
  if(Log) return(ln_Const) else return(exp(ln_Const))
}

# calculates the mathematical odds of hitting a specific number of caught turtles
dCMP <- function( x, lambda, mu, nu, log=TRUE, tol=0.01, iter.max=200 ){
  if(missing(mu) & !missing(lambda)) loglike = x*log(lambda) - nu*lfactorial(x) - compute_CMP_constant(Lambda=lambda, Nu=nu, Mu=NA, Tol=tol, Max=iter.max, Log=TRUE, Type="Z")
  if(!missing(mu) & missing(lambda)) loglike = nu*x*log(mu) - nu*lfactorial(x) - compute_CMP_constant(Lambda=NA, Nu=nu, Mu=mu, Tol=tol, Max=iter.max, Log=TRUE, Type="S")
  if(log) return(loglike) else return(exp(loglike))
}

# random number generator to simulate a realistic fishing year, based on the odds above
rCMP <- function( n, lambda, mu, nu, tol=0.01, x_max=200 ){
  loglike_x = rep(NA, x_max+1)
  for( x in 0:x_max ){
    if(missing(mu) & !missing(lambda)) loglike_x[x+1] = dCMP( x=x, lambda=lambda, nu=nu, log=TRUE, tol=tol, iter.max=x_max)
    if(!missing(mu) & missing(lambda)) loglike_x[x+1] = dCMP( x=x, mu=mu, nu=nu, log=TRUE, tol=tol, iter.max=x_max)
  }
  return(sample(x=0:x_max, size=n, replace=TRUE, prob=exp(loglike_x)))
}

# --- PRIMARY COHORT DATA MATH TRANSFORMS ---
inv_logit  <- function(x) { exp(x) / (1 + exp(x)) }
safe_logit <- function(x) { x_safe <- pmax(0.001, pmin(0.999, x)); log(x_safe / (1 - x_safe)) }

Defining the Species and Threats
Before projecting into the future, we define the life-history parameters and the conservation interventions we plan to test.
#| label: biology-constants
#| code-fold: false

# =========================================================================================
# 1. Baseline demography and multi-option intervention constants (EDIT for your population)
# =========================================================================================
# --- Simulation set up ---
set.seed(42) # Locks the randomizer seed for identical results every run
n_sims        <- 500   # Monte Carlo simulation sample size
horizon       <- 100   # Evaluation horizon window
hist_years    <- 2000:2020
n_hist_yrs    <- length(hist_years)

# --- Standard species demographic rates ---
# Eventually, the full version will allow you to calculate these based on data inputs
sp_clutch_freq <- 4.0    # Average nests per female per year
sp_remig_int   <- 3.0    # Adult female remigration interval (years)
sp_ane_pj      <- 0.82   # Juvenile age-class annual survival
sp_ane_pa      <- 0.92   # Mature adult female annual survival
sp_linf        <- 105.0  # Asymptotic maximum Straight Carapace Length (SCL, cm)
sp_k           <- 0.12   # VBGF somatic growth parameter
sp_tknot       <- -1.5   # Theoretical age at length zero (t_0)
sp_lmat        <- 95.0   # Length constraint proxy triggering maturity transition
sp_sig_mat     <- 4.5    # Logistic slope of maturation ogive curve
sp_max_age     <- 50.0   # Asymptotic maximum age lifespan ceiling
sp_pf          <- 0.68   # Sex ratio split (percent female)

# --- Threat parameter constants (Fishery baseline) ---
cmp_mu         <- 15.0   # Status quo mean annual longline interaction rate
cmp_nu         <- 0.15   # CMP dispersion factor

# --- Multi-option conservation portfolio parameters ---
# These need to be agreed upon with ZS and SM; should we pick a single species of turtle?
# Option A: Nest Protection (Caging)
optA_nests_saved      <- 35     # Extra nests saved annually from beach predators
sp_clutch_size        <- 95     # Mean eggs deposited per clutch
sp_hatch_success      <- 0.72   # Hatching success probability (under protection)
sp_hatch_to_yearling  <- 0.025  # First-year pelagic survival rate baseline

# Option B: Headstarting Program
optB_headstarts_released <- 150 # Yearling juveniles reared and released annually, potential to do 3 months, 6 months, etc.
optB_release_age         <- 2.0 # Age at release (skips early mortality)

# Option C: Cessation of Poaching (nesting adults)
optC_adults_saved        <- 6   # Mature nesting females rescued from harvest annually

# Option D: Gear Avoidance Modifications (interaction rate reduction)
optD_cmp_mu_mitigated    <- 4.5 # Mitigated interaction rate (e.g., bait/depth shifts)

# Option E: Safe handling practices (mortality rate reduction)
optE_mort_min            <- 0.05 # Lower post-release mortality bounds via circle hooks
optE_mort_max            <- 0.18 # Upper post-release mortality bounds via circle hooks

# Save environment for the next page
save.image("pva_state.RData")

title: Phase III: Retrospective Demographics (ANE)
text:
#| label: load-state
#| echo: false
load("pva_state.RData")
library(dplyr)
To accurately project population impacts, we must calculate the “ghost life” of historic bycatch. This symmetric inversion logic maps carapace length to an explicit age class to find an individual’s Adult Nester Equivalent (ANE).
#| label: ane-calculator
#| code-summary: "Retrospective Demographic Conversions (ANE)"

# ===============================
# 4. The Age and ANE Calculator
# ===============================
message(">>> Processing retrospective demographic historical conversions...")

# combining the data into a master spreadsheet of every interaction, lengths/mortalities when known, or filled in
all_turtles <- data.frame()
for(yr in unique(mock_safe$Year)) {
  obs_yr    <- mock_observer %>% filter(Year == yr)
  total_est <- mock_safe %>% filter(Year == yr) %>% pull(Total_Est)
  n_unobs   <- max(0, total_est - nrow(obs_yr))

  if(nrow(obs_yr) > 0) {
    all_turtles <- rbind(all_turtles, data.frame(Year = yr, Length = obs_yr$SCL_raw, Mortality = obs_yr$M_mu))
  }
  if(n_unobs > 0) {
    all_turtles <- rbind(all_turtles, data.frame(Year = yr, Length = rnorm(n_unobs, mean = 74, sd = 7), Mortality = runif(n_unobs, 0.45, 0.55)))
  }
}

# the loop of the dead, processing every turtle and tracks its ghost life in the future
sp_ledger <- data.frame()
for (i in 1:nrow(all_turtles)) {
  c_year <- all_turtles$Year[i]

  # symmetric inversion logic: Mapping carapace length to explicit age class
  age_start <- sp_tknot - (1 / sp_k) * log(1 - (pmin(all_turtles$Length[i], sp_linf - 0.1) / sp_linf))
  if(is.nan(age_start) || is.na(age_start)) age_start <- sp_max_age - 2

  # mapping the turtle's future path, what would its life have looked like?
  future_years <- seq(c_year, max(hist_years))
  l_y          <- length(future_years)
  if(l_y < 1) next

  # ages the turtle 1 year for each step and projects the growth (length) in those future years
  ages_traj <- seq(age_start, length.out = l_y, by = 1)
  lens_traj <- ifelse(ages_traj >= sp_max_age, rep(sp_linf * 0.99, l_y), sp_linf * (1 - exp(-sp_k * (ages_traj - sp_tknot))))

  # maturity tipping point, an adult cannot go back to a juvenile
  p_mat   <- 1.0 / (1.0 + exp(-(lens_traj - sp_lmat) / sp_sig_mat))
  p_binom <- rbinom(l_y, 1, p_mat)
  if(any(p_binom == 1)) p_binom[min(which(p_binom == 1)):l_y] <- 1

  # calculating cumulative survival, a turtle could have died naturally... would it still be alive?
  surv_vector <- cumprod((1 - p_binom) * sp_ane_pj + p_binom * sp_ane_pa)

  # final calculation that an individual animal is X proportion of an ANE
  sr <- surv_vector * p_binom * sp_pf * all_turtles$Mortality[i] * (1 / sp_remig_int)
  # probability that the turtle would still be alive, mature enough to lay eggs, female, severity of interaction, nesting frequency
  sp_ledger <- rbind(sp_ledger, data.frame(CalendarYear = future_years, ANE = sr))
}

#summarizes the historical damage, adds up every single fractional loss from every single turtle in a calendar year
final_historical_ane <- sp_ledger %>% group_by(CalendarYear) %>% summarise(Total_ANE = sum(ANE), .groups = "drop")

mock_posteriors_adj <- list(
  U = mock_posteriors$U + 0.0022, # adding back in the old fishery take (Martin et al., 2020)
  Q = mock_posteriors$Q * 0.94 # removing the man-made volatility of spikes (Martin et al., 2020)
)
# adding back in the turtles that should be alive to compare no fishery counterfactual (the ghosts)
start_pop_draws_adj <- start_pop_draws + (tail(final_historical_ane$Total_ANE, 1) * sp_remig_int)

save.image("pva_state.RData")

title: Phase V: Outcomes and Strategic Portfolios
text:
#| label: load-state
#| echo: false
load("pva_state.RData")
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
We aggregate our massive multi-track simulation to extract quantiles and visually assess which conservation strategies succeed.
#| label: plots
#| code-fold: false
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6

# ==============================================================================
# 6. Export outputs (figures and tables)
# ==============================================================================
message(">>> Composing conservation portfolio evaluation outputs")

yrs_proj <- (max(hist_years) + 1):(max(hist_years) + horizon)

generate_quantiles <- function(mat, label) {
  data.frame(
    Year = yrs_proj, Median = apply(mat, 2, median),
    L95 = apply(mat, 2, function(x) quantile(x, 0.025)),
    U95 = apply(mat, 2, function(x) quantile(x, 0.975)), Scenario = label
  )
}

# Consolidate core display trajectories
plot_df <- bind_rows(
  generate_quantiles(sim_notake, "Pristine Baseline (No Take)"),
  generate_quantiles(sim_take, "Status Quo Threats Only"),
  generate_quantiles(sim_optA, "Option A: Nest protection"),
  generate_quantiles(sim_optB, "Option B: Headstarting"),
  generate_quantiles(sim_optC, "Option C: Poaching cessation"),
  generate_quantiles(sim_combined, "Combined Recovery Portfolio")
)

# --- PVA overlay status quo with all options ---
p_overlay <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Year, y = Median, color = Scenario, fill = Scenario)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) + geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = L95, ymax = U95), alpha = 0.08, color = NA) +
  theme_bw() + theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.direction = "vertical") +
  labs(title = "Manuscript Fig 3: Multi-Option Conservation Portfolio Projections", y = "Total Mature Population Size")

print(p_overlay)
ggsave(filename = paste0(out_dir, "/Manuscript_Fig3_PVA_Portfolio_Overlay.png"), plot = p_overlay, width = 8, height = 6)

# --- Net loss (population change) ---
df_net_comb <- generate_quantiles(sim_combined - sim_take, "Net Portfolio Gain")
p_diff <- ggplot(df_net_comb, aes(x = Year, y = Median)) +
  geom_line(color = "darkorchid4", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = L95, ymax = U95), fill = "darkorchid", alpha = 0.20) +
  theme_bw() + labs(title = "Manuscript Fig 4: Net Combined Portfolio Payoff Timeline", y = "Net Abundance Gain (\u0394 Population)")

print(p_diff)
ggsave(filename = paste0(out_dir, "/Manuscript_Fig4_Net_Portfolio_Payoff.png"), plot = p_diff, width = 8, height = 4)

100-Year Scenario Scorecard
#| label: scorecard
#| code-fold: false

# --- All scenario 100 year comparison ---
scorecard_table <- data.frame(
  Management_Strategy_Track = c(
    "1. Pristine Baseline (No Take)", "2. Status Quo Realized Threats",
    "3. Option A (Nest Protections)", "4. Option B (Headstarting Enhancements)",
    "5. Option C (Poaching Cessation)", "6. Option D (Gear Avoidance Closures)",
    "7. Option E (Handling Severity Mitigation)", "8. Combined Strategic Portfolio"
  ),
  Median_100_Year_Abundance_Horizon = c(
    median(sim_notake[, horizon]), median(sim_take[, horizon]),
    median(sim_optA[, horizon]), median(sim_optB[, horizon]),
    median(sim_optC[, horizon]), median(sim_optD[, horizon]),
    median(sim_optE[, horizon]), median(sim_combined[, horizon])
  )
)

knitr::kable(scorecard_table, caption = "Table 1: Comprehensive PVA 100-Year Scorecard", digits = 1)
write.csv(scorecard_table, paste0(out_dir, "/Manuscript_Table1_Comprehensive_PVA_Scorecard.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

message("EXECUTION SUCCESSFUL. All multi-scenario outputs have been compiled and saved to: ", out_dir)
